IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some ivy that has grown on the pole and could interfere with the cables.

To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential work to remove this ivy. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely remove the ivy off the pole. By carefully planning this work in advance, we have been able to minimise the duration of the power interruption. 

Doing this helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable, as we appreciate your area has been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place on Sluice Road.

Doing this helps reduce the risk of future unplanned power cuts and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable, as we appreciate your area has been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place on Sluice Road.

This work is taking place on and around Common Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we need to connect a new substation to the electricity network. This equipment controls and delivers electricity into your area. To connect the new equipment and complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off. Once completed this will improve the reliability of the electricity network in your area, ensuring that the network can cope with the electricity usage in the area and reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. Please turn over for useful information. This work is taking place near Buckles Lane. Two way traffic lights will be in place to allow the works to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 17:07 - Thank you for your patience while our engineers investigated the cause of the power cut in the Swalecliffe area. We can now confirm that the issue is a fault on our underground electricity cabling and there are 2 properties affected, which we'll speak to directly. If you're without power and haven't been contacted, then we'll need to hear from you. Please call us on 08003163105 (free from landlines and mobiles).

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the unexpected outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 17:01 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Swalecliffe area, the estimated time of 21:30-22:30 hasn't changed. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse Sent by Adam at 16:55.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

If you're worried about elderly or ill neighbours, we can offer help and support to our vulnerable customers during a power cut. Find out more here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR. If you have any concerns, please call us on 08003163105
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 09:00 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Message: All power in the area should be back on. We’re really sorry for any disruption this caused.
Steps.Name: Power back on

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 07:16 - We can now confirm that all power in the St. Johns Road area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 06:16 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the St. Johns Road area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 10:30 and 11:30, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 07:36 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Byton Road area, the estimated time of 10:00-11:00 hasn't changed. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse Sent by Adam at 07:27.

Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
Steps.Name: We are restoring customers automatically

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way

Steps.Message: Engineers are now on site, carrying out investigations into the fault. Once they have completed their assessment, they will carry out repairs.
Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Message: All power in the area should be back on. We’re really sorry for any disruption this caused.
Steps.Name: Power back on

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We were investigating a high voltage overhead electricity line fault which caused a power cut in the local area.
PlannedIncidentReason: We're carrying out planned work in your area. For our engineers to carry it out safely they need to turn the power off. We're doing this work as it's essential to provide reliable electricity supplies to your area. We're sorry for any inconvenience caused and thank you for your patience.
MainMessage: We have repaired the problem with the electricity supply in your area. There may still be some tidying up to do, and any details have been passed to our Maintenance department, who will attend to it as soon as possible. Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message: Hi, just an update from Kasey at 13:16. Thanks for bearing with us today. Our staff are working hard to get you back on, and I just wanted to give you the latest from them. Our engineers have now managed to safely restore power to most of the affected properties, and have advised that only 3 customers in the High Street area of E6 2 are still without power. If your power is back on now, you can stop SMS updates on our website here - https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP. Otherwise, we're sorry your lights are still off; our estimated time frame of 13:30-14:30 hasn't changed and I'll continue to keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Talie at 12:29 - Our engineers have been able to divert electricity around the faulty part of the network, restoring power to some customers. We know your power may now be back on, however; we'll keep sending text updates to everyone until all the power to your area is on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 11:37 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 08:41 - We can now confirm that all power in the Grafton Way area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 07:57 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 07:47 - Our engineers have restored power to all 511 properties in the Ware SG12 0 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around an overhead cable fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Ware SG12 0 area to investigate the power cut affecting 511 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Great news - Our engineers have now restored power to all 16 customers in the Waldringfield area by diverting power around the overhead cable fault.

Sent by Bev at 07:56 - Our engineers have restored power to all 1300 properties in the Dunmow CM6 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around an underground cable fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.

Sent by Bev at 07:32 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an overhead cable fault that initially interrupted power to 1300 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 308 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 09:00-10:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.

Sent by Roland at 11:46 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that they need more time then initially anticipated to repair the fault. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 14:00 and 15:00

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 10:09 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 11:18 - Thanks for your patience today. Our engineers have now investigated the grounded overhead cable, and have deemed it safe to restore power back to all 362 properties in the Chelmsford CM3 5 area.

We're very sorry we had to turn your power off unexpectedly, and with so little warning. We usually provide much more notice if we can, but in a situation like this our first priority is the safety of the public and our staff.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. If your power is still off or if you have any questions, call our team on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Bev at 10:23 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Chelmsford CM3 5 area in the interest of the public's health and safety. This is so they can investigate reports of an overhead cable that is on the ground.

If in the area, please take care and stay at least 10m away from the cable. 

Until our engineers arrive on site and investigate, it's difficult to advise you a time frame. However, based on an average for this kind of issue, our estimated time to have you back on is between 12:30 and 13:30. 

We'll keep you updated with our progress, but in the meantime our team are always here to talk on 105 if you have any questions or concerns.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 10:02. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 12:40 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Talie at 12:20 - Hi - thanks for bearing with us, I know a power cut can be very disruptive. We've had an update from our staff that they have managed to safely divert some power, getting the lights back on for some of our customers. We'll keep sending text updates to everyone until all the power to your area is on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.

We're sorry your power has now been off for several hours and we're working as quickly as possible to get you back on.

We know you may be concerned about your phone battery running low, so here are some tips on how you can prolong battery life: Set your phone to power saving mode, reduce screen brightness, turn off WiFi, Bluetooth and GPS (if not in use), cut apps running in the background and if possible top up using a car charger or portable battery pack.

Sent by Talie at 14:11 - We can now confirm that all power in the Broadway area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

Sent by Talie at 14:04 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Engineers have arrived on site to investigate what we believe to be an issue at the local substation in the Broadway area. Using an average timescale for this type of issue, we're looking to have you back on between 15:30 and 16:30, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find.

We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Ebony and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected fault on our underground cables that affected 3974 properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. I'm pleased to report our staff have resolved this by diverting power to get your lights back on as soon as possible. Were your text messages delayed during this fault? Find out why here https://UKPN.short.gy/no-sms I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 13:45 - Our engineers have restored power to all 3974 properties in the Herne Bay CT6 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around an underground cable fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Roland at 13:06 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 11:55. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Hello, Kevin from the messaging team here. Thanks for your patience today. 

Our records show that some properties affected by today's power cut may have had other unexpected interruptions recently, and although we don't believe today's issue to be related, we are sorry for further disruption. Power cuts are fully investigated by our engineers, but can sometimes happen multiple times before we can safely find the issue and fix it.

You may not have had other power cuts recently, but if you have, our customer services team will try to update as many customers as possible about our investigations. 

Our staff are still working to get your power back on as quickly as they can, and we'll update you again soon.

MainMessage: We are currently experiencing problems with the electricity supply in your area. It is our intention to return to make a permanent repair as soon as we can.  I'm sorry, but we can't give you a time for this at present. Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 12:53 - Our engineers have restored power to all but one property in the London, SW1Y 6 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around the underground cable fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.

Steps.Message: Our customer support vehicle is now on site. It's located outside 4 Tonbridge Road and is ready to provide extra support to anyone affected by the power cut. If you have someone in your property or know of someone who needs extra care or cannot travel to the vehicle, please let us know by calling 08003163105 or tweet us @UKPowerNetworks. Sent by Kevin at 14:55. For a more precise location of this vehicle, just follow the link below. https://what3words.com/system.overdrive.jots
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Mcalpine Crescent area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Steps.Message:
Hi again. I know it can be uncomfortable having a power cut in higher temperatures, but as the evening draws in, there are ways to cool down.

Your property will still hold the heat built up over the day once the evening arrives, so we'd recommend opening windows and doors once the sun has gone in; outside temperatures should fall quickly and you can let some fresh air in.

Getting to sleep can also be a bit harder without power to run fans or air conditioning, but sleeping under a sheet instead of a duvet might help. As always, I'll keep you updated with news.

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 12:33. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 14:10 - We can now confirm that all power in the Craven Park Road area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 13:42- Just to let you know our staff have now arrived back on site in the Craven Park Road area. They are going to test our equipment thoroughly to check if the underlying issue can be located before they attempt to restore your power again.

I know having power cuts this often must be causing you a lot of problems, and our staff are doing everything they can to keep the power on. Our estimated time frame is currently between 17:00 and 18:00, although as we mentioned previously this can be subject to change.

We're keeping in touch with our staff regularly to make sure you have the best information to plan ahead. I'll update you as soon as we hear more from them.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 18:17 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Theo at 17:34 - We're very sorry we've had to switch off your electricity. Our engineers have turned off the power in the Sudbury CO10 8 area for safety due to a tree on our overhead network. Our estimated time to have your power back on is between 18:00 and 19:00, however, this may need to change later depending on how our works progress. If you'd like more information about why we sometimes have to switch power off to work; here's a short video https://UKPN.short.gy/Isol. We'll keep you updated with progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi again. I know it can be uncomfortable having a power cut in higher temperatures, but as the evening draws in, there are ways to cool down.

Your property will still hold the heat built up over the day once the evening arrives, so we'd recommend opening windows and doors once the sun has gone in; outside temperatures should fall quickly and you can let some fresh air in.

Getting to sleep can also be a bit harder without power to run fans or air conditioning, but sleeping under a sheet instead of a duvet might help. As always, I'll keep you updated with news. 

Sent by Adam at 18:49.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 15:40 - Our staff have now managed to restore power back to the Undercliff Road West area again.

They have thoroughly investigated our network equipment in the area, but the underlying network fault has not yet developed to a point for it to appear on any of their diagnostic tests. As such, they have replaced our substation equipment to restore power as quickly as possible.

I'm sorry this affected your power again today. This may happen again before we're able to pinpoint the issue and fix it, but if you'd like to talk to us about anything or if your power isn't on, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 14:52 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:

Our engineers have let us know that they have arrived onsite and assessed the work required to fix the underground network fault. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 19:00 and 20:00.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Kevin at 14:19 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is Birchington CT7 9 that initially interrupted power to 1081 properties. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. We're currently estimating power will be on between 17:00 and 18:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.

Sent by Kevin at 15:26 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that they have arrived onsite and assessed the work required to fix the underground network fault. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 19:00 and 20:00.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

Sent by Kevin at 14:19 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is Birchington CT7  9 that initially interrupted power to 1081 properties.

However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 400 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment.

We're currently estimating power will be on between 17:00 and 18:00, but this may change depending on what our staff find.

Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 15:57 - We can now confirm that all power in the Grayham Road area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 15:49 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by Adam at 15:32
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of a power cut at 13:55. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly.
Steps.Name: Reported

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way

Steps.Message: Engineers are now on site, carrying out investigations into the fault. Once they have completed their assessment, they will carry out repairs.
Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Message: All power in the area should be back on. We’re really sorry for any disruption this caused.
Steps.Name: Power back on

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 16:23 - Our engineers have restored power to all 39 properties in the Norwich, NR10 4 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around an overhead network fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 15:34 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Engineers have arrived on site in the Norwich, NR10 4 area to investigate an overhead cable fault. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off.

Using an average timescale for this type of issue we're looking to have you back on between 16:30 and 17:30, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We'll continue to update you with all of our progress.

Sent by Talie at 14:20.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our staff have now arrived on site in the Darenth Road area. They will start running tests at our substation to see how the local network is operating.

It's quite common for power to be restored briefly and go off again; this is our engineers using testing equipment to energise different sections of our network - this helps to narrow down any possible issues.

Our cabling network is often broken down by smaller sites, and our staff may also check these to test for any problems, and they may also walk the cabling route to see if there are any signs of malfunction that have appeared on the surface. Our time frame hasn't been adjusted, so for now I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us.

Sent by Adam at 15:17.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 14:25 - Our staff are on the way to the Darenth Road area, and our estimated time frame to have power back on is currently between 18:00 and 19:00. However, this could change once our staff have assessed the local network.

We believe the power cuts you have been experiencing are caused by a fault with one of the cables. Developing faults can start very small, and so can be hard to detect but each power cut usually makes it easier to see, which means our specialist staff can detect it, and work on the repair to prevent further outages.

I'll let you know once our teams arrive.

We know the time frame we initially estimated is now approaching. Our engineers are continuing their work to repair our overhead network in the Brandon, IP27 0/9 area.

As our staff are working with live electricity, we need to wait until it is safe for them to stop and contact us with an update.

We're sorry you've been impacted by this, and we'll provide you an update as soon as possible. Sent by Kevin at 18:36.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi again. I know it can be uncomfortable having a power cut in higher temperatures, but as the evening draws in, there are ways to cool down.

Your property will still hold the heat built up over the day once the evening arrives, so we'd recommend opening windows and doors once the sun has gone in; outside temperatures should fall quickly and you can let some fresh air in.

Getting to sleep can also be a bit harder without power to run fans or air conditioning, but sleeping under a sheet instead of a duvet might help. As always, I'll keep you updated with news. 

Sent by Kevin at 17:49.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 18:12 - Our engineers have just called in to advise us that we are awaiting the arrival of our specialist teams so they can begin to remove the trees from our overhead network.

This means it's going to take us past our current time so we are now working towards between 23:30 and 00:30 to have your power back on.

We know how disruptive being without power can be, we are working as fast as we can. If you're worried about anyone who may be more vulnerable in your property who is affected by todays outage, please don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent at 17:36 by Talie - More electricity has been diverted thanks to our ground team's continued work, and the lights are now safely back on to 164 further properties. If this is the case at your property, don't worry; I'll still keep you informed. Steps.Message:
Hi again. I know it can be uncomfortable having a power cut in higher temperatures, but as the evening draws in, there are ways to cool down.

Your property will still hold the heat built up over the day once the evening arrives, so we'd recommend opening windows and doors once the sun has gone in; outside temperatures should fall quickly and you can let some fresh air in.

Getting to sleep can also be a bit harder without power to run fans or air conditioning, but sleeping under a sheet instead of a duvet might help. As always, I'll keep you updated with news. 

Sent by Kevin at 18:32.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Hello again. I hope you're managing okay with the power being off; I know this might especially impact you while trying to keep cool. I know you, or someone in your property may be more vulnerable in high temperatures, and being without power may be more difficult. If you need any extra advice or assistance on how to stay safe in the heat, you can find the public health advice from the government website here https://UKPN.short.gy/heat. If you have any health concerns, please call NHS 111 before contacting us on 08003163105 to see how our team can assist you. I'll let you know once we have more news from our engineers. Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 18:51 - Thanks for bearing with us during the power cut, I know the lights being off is a worry.

Our staff have advised that they have extra resources attending to cut several trees away from the overhead lines before they can make repairs to our network. Due to the delay this has caused, they've advised a new time of 00:30 and 01:30 to have your power back on.

I know another delay is frustrating; our engineers always try to give us the best estimate that they can but due to the safety elements of working with live electricity, sometimes they have to adjust their repairs at short notice.

I'm sorry if this has caused you to change any plans you've made, but we'll be here to update you until the lights are safely back on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Sent by Luke at 18:28 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 176 customers in the London  Road area by diverting power around the high voltage fault. We're pleased that we have been able to restore power sooner than we estimated.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: We were investigating an electricity cable that was damaged, causing a power cut in the local area.

MainMessage: We are aware of an incident caused by LV 3rd Party Cable Damage affecting the electricity supply in your area. Our engineers are dealing with this, but we are sorry we can't give you a time for the restoration of supply at present.  Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 18:44 - We can now confirm that all power in the Paget Road area should be back on.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 17:36 - We're sorry your power has gone off again. We are aware there is an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts.

Due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself, making it difficult to locate. Our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, but this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix it.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 17:27 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Paget Road area, the estimated time of 19:00-20:00 hasn't changed. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse Sent by Adam at 17:05.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 17:34 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have assessed the repairs needed to the overhead cables that have come down onto the ground. Specialist teams are on their way and to give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 20:30 and 21:30.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 18:50 - Hi, thanks for bearing with us. I know on hot days like today, being without power means you may struggle to stay cool, and I wanted to give you some advice you might find helpful.

During the day keep external doors and windows closed, especially ones that face the sun, as heat will build up quickly. Also, keep your curtains and blinds drawn. In the evening, open windows to help a draft come in and if possible, sleep downstairs as it is often cooler should your power cut happen during the night.

We know it's not ideal, however, having a cold bath or shower can take the edge off the heat for a while. Please remember to drink plenty of water to stay hydrated and I will let you know more news soon.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Talie at 17:50 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that they have found some overhead cables that have come down in the area and will need to be re-erected. Specialist teams are on their way and to give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 00:00 and 01:00.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Talie at 17:32 - Hi - thanks for bearing with us, I know a power cut can be very disruptive. We've had an update from our staff that there are now 141 properties still affected by the overhead cable fault, as they have managed to safely divert some power, getting the lights back on for 213 of our customers. We'll keep sending text updates to everyone until all the power to your area is on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Luke at 18:31 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Green area, the estimated time of 20:30 and 21:20 hasn't changed. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse Sent by Luke at 17:49.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Kevin at 17:09 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the Green area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 20:00 and 21:00, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.

Steps.Message:
Sorry your power has gone off again today; our engineers previously diverted the electricity around the faulted part of the network remotely. However, this has not held and we believe there may be an underlying issue.

Our engineers are on site investigating, and we estimate to have power restored between 18:30 and 19:30. We have your information from the earlier power cut so will continue to send progress updates to you until all power is back on. Sent by Theo at 18:43.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 18:32 - We believe our engineers have been able to restore power to all 2292 customers in the Cambridge, CB24 8 area by diverting power around an overhead network fault.

If your power isn't back on, we'll need to hear from you. To report this, or to talk to us about anything else, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be worrying, but please rest assured that our staff work around the clock to keep the lights on. If you have any concerns or questions, give us a call any time.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 17:37. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
Steps.Name: We are restoring customers automatically

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way

Steps.Message: Engineers are now on site, carrying out investigations into the fault. Once they have completed their assessment, they will carry out repairs.
Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Luton, LU4 0 area to investigate the power cut affecting 564 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

MainMessage: We are aware of an incident caused by LV Fuse Replacement/Linking affecting the electricity supply in your area. Our engineers are dealing with this and we estimate that most supplies will be restored by 26-JUN-2026 22:00.  Please accept our sincere apologies for any inconvenience this may cause.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 18:47 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Kennington Lane area, the estimated time of 21:30-22:30 hasn't changed. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. We'll update you with all of our progress, but if you need to speak to us, call our team on 08003163105 any time. If you would like more information about what we think the fault is; here's a short YouTube video https://UKPN.short.gy/fuse Sent by Adam at 18:37.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 18:31 - Some customers have told us that they want to be notified about a power cut before it happens. We're sorry we couldn't give you advance notice about this power cut. The issue happened suddenly and without warning, leaving us with no time to alert customers.
Our electricity network is regularly inspected and maintained to help prevent outages, but occasionally an unexpected fault can lead to an unplanned power cut. It's similar to a car that's been fully serviced and passed its MOT but still breaks down without warning.
Thank you for your understanding and we will keep you updated about todays outage until you are restored.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Adam at 18:12 - Engineers are on their way. At this point we think the issue is a fault with equipment in your local substation in the Kennington Lane area. We didn't know this was going to happen, so time frames we provide throughout are estimated and are subject to change.

Our estimated time to have power back on is currently between 21:30 and 22:30, but after we arrive and assess the situation this may change. The reason for this is there can sometimes be a more complex underlying issue found by our engineer's tests which may take longer, and if not we may get power back on sooner.

All of our texts are written and sent personally by our team, and we'll let you know once our staff arrive.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of a power cut at 17:43. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly.
Steps.Name: Reported

Steps.Message: We have engineers on their way. As this is affecting your area, they will not need to attend your property. Rest assured once they arrive, they’ll work as quickly as is safely possible to get your power back on.
Steps.Name: Engineers on their way

Steps.Message: Engineers are now on site, carrying out investigations into the fault. Once they have completed their assessment, they will carry out repairs.
Steps.Name: Investigating & fixing

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Maidstone area to investigate the power cut affecting 309 properties. Specialist engineers are also working to divert power remotely, and this can cause your lights to go on and off. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive, even more so if you are vulnerable. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress but for any concerns or questions, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. For more information on how we support vulnerable customers just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by Luke at 18:24 - Our engineers are on their way. We believe the issue is an underground fault that initially interrupted power to 437 properties. However, we have managed to divert power back to some properties, so 309 properties are still affected. Specialist engineers from our control room will continue to divert power remotely, so your power may come back on at any point. Sometimes we can't restore everyone this way, so we're also sending staff to check our equipment. We're currently estimating power will be on between 21:00 and 22:00 but this may change depending on what our staff find. Power back on? To stop receiving texts, follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Otherwise, we'll keep you updated.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 17:46. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Sent by Talie at 18:32 - We're sorry your power has gone off again today. Our engineers investigated the fault earlier but it seems there may be a bigger underlying issue which is causing our substation equipment to operate repeatedly.

Our engineers will be back on site as quickly as possible and using an average time frame, we are aiming to have all power back on between 22:00 and 23:00. We'll keep you updated with all of our progress.

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage is affecting multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you'd expect. We have already sent staff to the area, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting the electricity around the faulty section. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to restore as much power as we can.

The engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will locate and repair the issue, which will take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing, but we'll always try to give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team
